prompt:
A 250 mL amber plastic bottle with a secure, child-resistant cap stands on a lab bench, marked only with a simple droplet-and-bubbles hazard icon instead of readable text. The bottle is slightly translucent so that the clear liquid inside is just visible. Nearby, a pair of nitrile gloves and a small brush or cloth suggest that the 3% hydrogen peroxide is used for sanitizing hydroponic equipment, while a hydroponic tub and tubing appear softly in the background.

The setting is a near-future solarpunk hydroponics workspace with soft, even lighting, neatly coiled hoses, and a couple of healthy plants growing in a system behind the bottle. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with subtle stylization, no logos or readable labels, and a composition that centers the amber bottle and safety-aware surroundings while the rest of the environment stays gently out of focus, reinforcing safe, careful chemical handling.
